content.text:
```json
{
  "approved": true,
  "feedback": "The fix has been correctly and completely implemented. In src/app/render-browse.ts, the code now builds the table with an empty-string Actions header (buildTable(['Name','Size','Modified',''])), which produces a visually empty <th> (buildTable sets textContent = label, and an empty string yields no visible text), and then immediately sets aria-label='Actions' on that last header cell via table.querySelector('thead th:last-child')?.setAttribute('aria-label','Actions'). This matches the preferred approach in the fix instructions exactly: the header remains visually blank while still announcing 'Actions' to assistive tech. The change is minimal and targeted, buildTable in dom-builders.ts is left unchanged (empty string still produces an empty <th>), the browse-table class is preserved, and the updated code comment accurately describes the rationale. No dead code, no scope expansion, and the pattern (setAttribute for aria-* attributes) is consistent with the rest of the codebase (e.g., the row-menu button uses setAttribute for aria-label/aria-haspopup/aria-expanded).",
  "issues": []
}
```
